message:
refactor(core): asupersync compat refinements and snapshot_engine borrow cleanup

runtime.rs:
- Rustfmt line-length normalization for get_max_seq() and cursors.insert()
  call chains (cosmetic only; no logic changes)

snapshot_engine.rs:
- Add #[cfg_attr(feature = "asupersync-runtime", allow(unused_mut))] to
  suppress the unused-mut warning on trigger_rx under asupersync where the
  channel receiver is consumed differently
- Flatten nested borrow scope in ensure_or_update_session(): replace the
  nested { let guard = self.session_id.read().await; ... } block with
  a single let existing_session_id = { ... .clone() }; followed by a
  flat if-let.  This eliminates the inner borrow scope that held the
  RwLock guard across an await point (the spawn_blocking call), which
  would cause lifetime issues under asupersync's stricter borrow model

tailer.rs:
- Restructure cfg-gated permit reservation in poll_pane to hoist the
  context binding (let reserve_cx = crate::cx::for_testing()) into its
  own cfg-gated let statement rather than wrapping the entire match in
  a block expression.  This produces cleaner cfg layering: two adjacent
  cfg-gated let-bindings (one for context, one for permit) rather than
  one cfg-gated block vs one cfg-gated match.  Applied to both the
  overflow-gap and normal capture code paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
